sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The gnn_event class is a specialization of the event_template designed to handle the output of a Graph Neural Network (GNN). It reads the GNN's predictions and reconstructs particle objects (tops and Z' primes) based on these predictions.

sect1.title: The gnn_event Class
sect1.para: The gnn_event class inherits from event_template and implements its virtual methods to provide event-specific functionality.

listitem.para: gnn_event(): The constructor initializes the event by setting its name, defining the data leaves to be read from the input tree, and registering the particle types that are expected in the event.
~gnn_event(): The destructor deregisters the particle collections created during the event processing.
event_template* clone(): Creates a new instance of the gnn_event.
void build(element_t* el): Populates the event's data members with the GNN predictions and truth information from the input data element.

void CompileEvent(): This is the core method of the class. It reconstructs the event by clustering the GNN nodes into particle objects based on the predicted edge scores. This includes:
Clustering: A PageRank-based clustering algorithm is used to group nodes into top and Z' candidates based on the edge_top_scores and edge_res_scores respectively.
Truth Reconstruction: The same clustering is applied to the truth-level edge information (t_edge_top and t_edge_res) to reconstruct the true top and Z' particles.
Particle Creation: For each cluster, a top or zprime particle is created, and its kinematic properties are calculated by summing the four-vectors of its constituent nodes.
Vectorization: The reconstructed and truth particle collections are vectorized for easier access.

sect2.title: GNN Predictions
sect2.para:
The gnn_event class stores the following GNN predictions:
edge_res_scores: The predicted probability of each edge being part of a Z' decay.
edge_top_scores: The predicted probability of each edge being part of a top quark decay.
ntops_scores: The predicted probability distribution for the number of top quarks in the event.
signal_scores: The predicted probability of the event being a signal event.
p_signal: The predicted signal class (boolean).
s_signal: The signal score.
p_ntops: The predicted number of top quarks.
s_ntops: The score for the predicted number of top quarks.

sect2.title: Truth Information
sect2.para:
The gnn_event class also stores the following truth information:
t_ntops: The true number of top quarks.
t_signal: The true signal label.
t_edge_res: The true Z' edge labels.
t_edge_top: The true top edge labels.

listitem.para: r_zprime: A vector of reconstructed zprime particles.
r_tops: A vector of reconstructed top particles.
t_zprime: A vector of truth-level zprime particles.
t_tops: A vector of truth-level top particles.
event_particles: A vector of the input particle_gnn nodes to the GNN.
